stem: Five angles meet at a point: $(2x + 10)°$, $(3x - 20)°$, $(x + 40)°$, $(4x - 30)°$, and $y°$. Two of these angles are vertical angles: $(2x + 10)°$ and $(x + 40)°$. What is the value of $y$?
explanation: Since $(2x+10)°$ and $(x+40)°$ are vertical angles, they are equal: $2x + 10 = x + 40$, so $x = 30$. The five angles around a point sum to $360°$: $(2(30)+10) + (3(30)-20) + (30+40) + (4(30)-30) + y = 360$. This gives $70 + 70 + 70 + 90 + y = 360$, so $300 + y = 360$, thus $y = 60$.

distractorRationale.D: Found one of the other angles ($70°$) instead of $y$.
